data.title: Brixmor prices $400M of 4.85% senior notes due 2033
data.description:
• Brixmor Operating Partnership, the operating arm of Brixmor Property Group (NYSE:BRX), has priced a $400M offering of 4.850% senior notes due 2033.
  • The notes were issued at 99.849% of par value, with interest payable semi-annually starting February 15, 2026.
  • The notes will mature on February 15, 2033.
  • Use of proceeds includes general corporate purposes, potentially including debt repayment.
  • The offering is expected to close on September 9, 2025.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Costo posts another strong sales month in August
data.description:
Costco Wholesale Corporation (NASDAQ:COST) reported comparable sales for the retail month of August were up 6.3%, including a 6.1% increase in the U.S., a 6.8% rise in Canada, and a 6.7% gain for other international markets. E-commerce comparable sales shot up 18.3% during the quarter.

Costco (NASDAQ:COST) said comparable traffic was 4.1% higher during the month.

After stripping out the impact of foreign exchange and gas prices, comparable sales were up 6.9% during the month, including an increase of 6.7% in the U.S. The core sales increase was just off the 7.0% pace seen in August and higher than the 5.8% increase in July.

Total sales for the four-week period that ended on August 31 were up 8.7% to $19.83 billion to easily outpace the retail sector average. 

The retailer noted strong results for the fresh food category in August, which was up mid- to high single digits. The jewelry and garden categories were also strong during the month compared to a year ago.

Costco currently operates 914 warehouses, including 629 in the United States and Puerto Rico, 110 in Canada, 42 in Mexico, 37 in Japan, 29 in the United Kingdom, 20 in Korea, 15 in Australia, 14 in Taiwan, seven in China, five in Spain, two in France, two in Sweden, and one each in Iceland, and New Zealand. Costco also operates e-commerce sites in the U.S., Canada, the U.K., Mexico, Korea, Taiwan, Japan, and Australia.

Shares of Costco (COST) rose 0.1% in postmarket trading to $956.81 after rallying 0.6% during the regular session.

data.title: Guidewire Software Non-GAAP EPS of $0.84 beats by $0.21, revenue of $356.6M beats by $18.75M
data.description:
• Guidewire Software press release (NYSE:GWRE): FQ4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.84 beats by $0.21.
  • Revenue of $356.6M (+22.3% Y/Y) beats by $18.75M.
  • Shares +3.62%.

  • Business Outlook

    Guidewire is issuing the following outlook for the first quarter of fiscal year 2026 based on current expectations:

    • Ending ARR between $1,048 million and $1,054 million
    • Total revenue between $315 million and $321 million
    • Operating income between $1 million and $7 million
    • Non-GAAP operating income between $47 million and $53 million

    Guidewire is issuing the following outlook for fiscal year 2026 based on current expectations:

    • Ending ARR between $1,210 million and $1,220 million
    • Total revenue between $1,385 million and $1,405 million
    • Operating income between $68 million and $88 million
    • Non-GAAP operating income between $259 million and $279 million
    • Operating cash flow between $350 million and $370 million
    • Q3 revenue consensus is $301.39M
    • FY revenue consensus is $1.35B

data.title: Zumiez GAAP EPS of -$0.06 beats by $0.05, revenue of $214.27M beats by $3.74M
data.description:
• Zumiez press release (NASDAQ:ZUMZ): Q2 GAAP EPS of -$0.06 beats by $0.05.
  • Revenue of $214.27M (+1.9% Y/Y) beats by $3.74M.

  • The company is introducing guidance for the three months ending November 1, 2025. Net sales are projected to be in the range of $232 to $237 million including anticipated comparable sales growth of between 5.5% and 7.5%. Consolidated operating margins are expected to be between 2.3% and 3.3% resulting in earnings per diluted share of approximately $0.19 to $0.29.  

    The Company currently intends to open approximately 6 new stores in fiscal 2025, including up to 5 stores in North America and 1 store in Australia.

  • Shares +3.79% AH.

data.title: Wall Street rallied to finish in the green ahead of Friday's payrolls report
data.description:
Wall Street traded higher on Thursday as investors weighed fresh jobs data for clues on the labor market’s strength.

The tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was the day's best performer as it added 0.9%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) climbed up by 0.8%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) was able to conclude higher by 0.7%.

On a sector-by-sector basis, 10 of the 11 S&P segments finished trading in positive territory, led by Consumer Discretionary and Communication Services. At the other end of the spectrum, Utilities lagged the most on Thursday. 

In the bond market, yields were under some slight pressure as the longer-end U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) dipped 5 basis points to 4.16%, and the shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) pushed down 3 basis points to 3.58%.

On the economic front, job cuts climbed 39% in August, boosting the YTD total higher than all of 2024. Also, U.S. private sector employment continued softening, slightly more than expected, in August. 

Initial jobless claims rose slightly more than expected in the past week. Productivity was revised up, and labor costs were revised lower in second Q2 prints. 

The U.S. goods and services trade deficit widened more than expected in July.

Additionally, the U.S. ISM Services Index rose to 52.0 in August from 50.1 in July, exceeding the 50.9 consensus, according to data released by the Institute for Supply Management.

"As tomorrow is the first Friday of the month, all eyes will be on tomorrow’s jobs report. Unlike in August when the most important number was the unemployment rate, now, all eyes are on the payroll number, as this market is all about job creation,” Seeking Alpha analyst Leo Nelissen stated.

“After all, if the job market gets bad enough, the Fed may be forced to cut rates more aggressively, regardless of inflation. However, if numbers are too bad, it may suggest something worse is on the horizon, which wouldn’t be bullish,” Nelissen added.

On more of the political side, President Donald Trump urged the Supreme Court to quickly accept and rule on an appeal aiming to overturn lower court rulings that deemed most of his tariffs illegal.

As for stocks that were on the move, shares of American Eagle Outfitters (AEO) surged 38.1% after the apparel maker said the advertising campaign starring actress Sydney Sweeney gave its brand and business a reset.

data.title: Paramount Skydance and Legendary sign distribution deal
data.description:
Paramount Skydance (NASDAQ:PSKY) inked a three-year deal with Legendary Entertainment to distribute upcoming films, an agreement that would essentially double Paramount's (NASDAQ:PSKY) output to 20 theatrical releases a year beginning with Legendary’s Street Fighter film scheduled for an October 2026 release.

Since the franchise began in 1987, Street Fighter has sold over 49 million units worldwide to become one of the most popular and highest-grossing video game franchises of all time.

Talks between Paramount Skydance (NASDAQ:PSKY) and Legendary began last month after Legendary’s deal with Sony Pictures was mutually terminated. While Legendary’s new pact with Paramount Skydance (PSKY) covers upcoming releases, the studio will still distribute select sequels through former partner Warner Bros. (WBD), which retains franchise rights to Minecraft, Dune, and Godzilla.

Earlier this week, Paramount Skydance (PSKY) announced a deal with Activision Blizzard (MSFT) to make a film based on the video game Call of Duty, as well as a deal for the rights to the Ultimate Fighting Championship for the next seven years.
